item.title: Taylor Farms pulls iceberg lettuce linked to cyclosporiasis outbreak
item.description: Taylor Farms said none of its branded salads or kits contain the iceberg lettuce associated with the outbreak.

item.title: Iran attacks U.S. allies as 3 American soldiers killed in escalating war
item.description: Iran attacks Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan and a ship in the Strait of Hormuz as at least 3 U.S. forces are killed and dozens wounded in the escalating conflict.

item.title: Younger Democrats push for committee leadership term limits, generational change
item.description: A group of younger Democrats is pushing for term limits for committee leadership roles, citing gerontocracy as a major issue hindering American democracy. Majority Democrats' Rodericka Applewhaite explains why she believes age should carry less weight when considering political leadership roles.

item.title: Appeals court denies Biden bid to block release of tapes with biographer, for now
item.description: An appeals court denied former President Joe Biden's attempt to keep the Justice Department from giving a conservative think tank redacted transcripts and audio recordings of conversations with his biographer.

item.title: Novo Nordisk sues Eli Lilly, accusing it of false advertising
item.description: The lawsuit alleges that Eli Lilly misled consumers by using outdated clinical trial data to make its drugs appear more effective than Novo's.

item.title: FDA says lettuce still likely source of cyclospora outbreak despite false positive test
item.description: The FDA said signs still point to Taylor Farms shredded iceberg lettuce as the likely culprit of the cyclospora outbreak despite an earlier test on a sample that was a false positive. CBS News' Carter Evans reports.

item.title: U.S. soldier killed in Iraq identified as 30-year-old from North Carolina
item.description: Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton, 30, of North Carolina, was "decommissioning ammunition and an accident happened," Secretary of State Marco Rubio said.

item.title: Hegseth to testify on Capitol Hill as Democrats press for more Iran war updates
item.description: Nearly 100 U.S. military service members were injured by Iran's strikes in the last month, CBS News has learned. This comes as the war with Iran falls under increased scrutiny on Capitol Hill. CBS News' Aaron Navarro reports.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: What are the best ways to lower your credit card interest rates now? Here's what some experts think.
item.description: Average credit card rates are sitting near 22% and quietly draining wallets. Here's how to fight back now.

item.title: 2 soldiers from Texas and Hawaii killed in Iran strike in Jordan; 1 still missing
item.description: U.S. forces launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran at the direction of President Trump following the deaths of two U.S. soldiers in an Iranian strike in Jordan.

item.title: Oprah gives inside look at her expanding podcast and reveals best guest she's ever had
item.description: Oprah Winfrey announced she's expanding her podcast, "The Oprah Podcast." Starting on Tuesday, two new episodes will drop weekly. She spoke to "CBS Mornings" co-host Gayle King about her decision and revealed more about the guests she's had appear on the podcast.

item.title: More than three-quarters of Americans plan to travel this summer. Here's how to budget
item.description: CBS News business analyst Jill Schlesinger talks to "CBS Mornings" about ways to stretch your summer vacation budget and some costly mistakes to avoid.

item.title: Man recalls moment he was attacked by bison at Yellowstone: "He could have gored me"
item.description: The man who was attacked by a bison at Yellowstone National Park earlier this month is speaking out for the first time about the terrifying encounter. Carl Isom-McDaniel, 65, and his grandson were on a walk when the bison suddenly charged at them and flipped McDaniel 8 feet into the air. Jonathan Vigliotti reports.

item.title: Iran retaliates after U.S. military carries out 10th night of strikes
item.description: The U.S. carried out its 10th consecutive night of strikes against Iran. Tehran said it retaliated by targeting U.S. assets in Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata reports.

item.title: Back-to-school shopping already? Here's how to save on the essentials
item.description: Back-to-school shopping is already approaching and you may qualify for extra savings thanks to tax-free holidays. Jo Ling Kent explains.

item.title: What we know about the Army veteran accused of setting off fireworks outside NYC federal building
item.description: A U.S. Army veteran was arrested after he set off fireworks and started a fire outside of a Manhattan building, which houses federal agencies, including ICE, the FBI said. CBS News' Anna Schecter has more details.

item.title: Prosecutors to present evidence at hearing for musician D4vd
item.description: Musician D4vd will be in court in Los Angeles on Tuesday for a preliminary hearing in a murder case. Prosecutors will present evidence, claiming he is responsible for the death of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez. Lilia Luciano reports.

item.title: Darline Graham says she'll run for full Senate term after death of her brother, Lindsey Graham
item.description: South Carolina Sen. Darline Graham, Lindsey Graham's sister, says she'll run for her late brother's seat and look to win the Republican nomination in a special election next month. Nikole Killion has more.

item.title: Darline Graham joins race for Lindsey Graham's South Carolina Senate seat with backing of Trump
item.description: After the death of South Carolina Republican Sen. Lindsey Graham, his sister, Darline Graham, stepped in to serve out the remainder of his term. Now, she says she plans to run for the full term. CBS News' Fin Gómez has more details.

item.title: Nolan Wells remembered amid probe into his death: "We'll continue to fight for you"
item.description: Family and friends in Mississippi gathered to remember the life of Nolan Wells two weeks after the 18-year-old's mysterious death. Skyler Henry reports.

item.title: Ford recalls nearly 388,000 vehicles over seat issue
item.description: The recall affects certain 2020-2027 Aviator and 2020-2026 Explorer vehicles.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha threatens Gulf Coast with strong winds and rain
item.description: The Gulf Coast is bracing for heavy wind and rain from Tropical Storm Bertha. CBS News' Lana Zak reports on the storm's forecast.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha strengthens, threatening Gulf Coast. See its path.
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is the second named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season.

item.title: Tourists stabbed near famed Acropolis in Athens, Greece
item.description: A man stabbed two tourists near the ancient Acropolis in Athens, Greece's top tourist attraction, police said.

item.title: FBI agents told to stand down on ICE assault probes; DOJ denies policy shift
item.description: Homeland Security Investigations is an arm of ICE, meaning the immigration enforcement agency would effectively be investigating incidents involving its own agents.

item.title: Trump to impose 50% tariffs on Canadian hockey sticks, alcohol, other goods
item.description: President Trump is imposing 50% tariffs on a range of Canadian goods, from hockey equipment to alcoholic beverages, escalating the trade dispute between the two countries.

item.title: Mark Warner says he'll vote against Trump pick Jay Clayton as DNI
item.description: Sen. Mark Warner said he'll vote against Jay Clayton as director of national intelligence, citing disappointment in his testimony at a confirmation hearing last week despite his previous optimism about the nominee.

item.title: 2 families sue hospital after Colorado man claims he was switched at birth
item.description: Two years ago, Jeremy Morrison took a DNA test and found out his parents were not biological actual parents.

item.title: Apollo 11: Behind The Scenes
item.description: While the three astronauts of Apollo 11 received the bulk of the attention for landing on the moon in 1969, 400,000 people worked behind the scenes to make it happen. Randall Pinkston reports.
